requirements.text: The adaptive headlight system shall adjust beam direction within 50ms of steering input detection.
The lighting control unit shall remain operational between -40°C and 125°C ambient temperature.
The system shall comply with ECE Regulation 123 for adaptive front lighting systems.
The headlight system shall achieve ASIL-B functional safety classification.
The LED driver circuit shall supply a minimum of 1000 lumens per headlight unit.
The actuator motor shall achieve beam adjustment with a positional accuracy of ±0.1 degrees.
The hardware components shall operate within a voltage range of 9V to 16V DC.
The temperature sensor shall have a measurement accuracy of ±2°C across the full operating range.
The beam control algorithm shall process steering angle sensor data at a minimum frequency of 100Hz.
The software shall implement a watchdog timer with a timeout period of 10ms.
The firmware shall log all fault events to non-volatile memory within 5ms of detection.
The control software shall operate within 8°C to 120°C — NOTE: inconsistent with REQ-SYS-002.
In case of actuator failure, the system shall default to a fixed straight-ahead beam position within 100ms.
requirements.safety_reason: Fail-safe behavior is critical for safety.

requirements.text: The system shall detect LED failure and activate a driver warning within 2 seconds.
requirements.safety_reason: Fault detection and human safety impact are relevant.

requirements.text: All safety-relevant software components shall have 100% MC/DC coverage in testing.
requirements.safety_reason: Ensures comprehensive fault coverage and safety.

requirements.text: The beam adjustment latency shall be verified under simulated steering inputs at -20°C, 25°C, and 85°C.
Voltage tolerance testing shall be performed at 9V, 12V, and 16V input levels.

safety_assessments.disclaimer: SUGGESTIONS ONLY — All ASIL assignments require human expert review per ISO 26262

assessments.rationale: Headlights can cause glare and reduce visibility, leading to accidents.
Watchdog timer failure could lead to system hang, affecting overall safety.
Failure to log faults could prevent timely detection and mitigation.
Actuator failure can lead to reduced visibility, increasing accident risk.
LED failure can reduce visibility and increase driver workload.
Incomplete test coverage can lead to undetected faults.

traceability_gaps.description: No corresponding software requirement for the actuator motor's positional accuracy.
traceability_gaps.suggested_action: Create a software requirement such as 'The control software shall ensure that the actuator motor achieves a beam adjustment with a positional accuracy of ±0.1 degrees.'

traceability_gaps.description: No corresponding test requirement for the software's temperature operating range.
traceability_gaps.suggested_action: Create a test requirement such as 'The control software shall be tested for operation within the voltage range of 8V to 120V DC.'

traceability_gaps.description: No acceptance criterion specified for the time taken to default to a fixed straight-ahead beam position in case of actuator failure.
traceability_gaps.suggested_action: Add an acceptance criterion such as 'The system shall default to a fixed straight-ahead beam position within 100ms of actuator failure detection.'

traceability_gaps.description: No acceptance criterion specified for the time taken to detect LED failure and activate a driver warning.
traceability_gaps.suggested_action: Add an acceptance criterion such as 'The system shall detect LED failure and activate a driver warning within 2 seconds of failure.'

traceability_gaps.description: No acceptance criterion specified for the 100% MC/DC coverage in testing of safety-relevant software components.
traceability_gaps.suggested_action: Add an acceptance criterion such as 'All safety-relevant software components shall have 100% MC/DC coverage in their respective test scenarios.'

audit_log.input: Document: LGT-REQ-001 — Valeo Lighting System Requirements
